# Capability Matrix Snapshot

**Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
**Generated by:** Objective 10 cross-database conformance gate
**Previous snapshot:** (none — first snapshot)

This matrix is a versioned artifact. It is updated when adapter capabilities change. Removing or downgrading a declared capability is a breaking change requiring a major version bump on the affected adapter package (per ADR-0093).

---

## Legend

| Symbol | Meaning                                                   |
| ------ | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| ✅     | Full support — passes conformance tests                   |
| ⚠️     | Partial support — supported with caveats documented below |
| ❌     | Not supported — operations rejected with clear error      |
| N/A    | Not applicable to this database's paradigm                |

---

## Core Operations

| Capability               | PostgreSQL | MSSQL | MongoDB | Notes |
| ------------------------ | ---------- | ----- | ------- | ----- |
| Insert                   | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |       |
| Read by ID               | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |       |
| Read with filters        | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |       |
| Update (optimistic lock) | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |       |
| Delete                   | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |       |
| Cursor pagination        | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |       |
| Workspace scoping        | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |       |

## Transactions

| Capability                                | PostgreSQL | MSSQL | MongoDB | Notes                                        |
| ----------------------------------------- | ---------- | ----- | ------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| Single-entity atomic write                | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |                                              |
| Multi-entity transaction                  | ✅         | ✅    | ⚠️      | MongoDB: requires replica set; session-based |
| Distributed transaction                   | ❌         | ❌    | ❌      | Out of scope for v1                          |
| Optimistic concurrency conflict detection | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |                                              |
| Pessimistic locking (withLock)            | ✅         | ✅    | ⚠️      | MongoDB: emulated via findAndModify          |

## Audit Log

| Capability               | PostgreSQL | MSSQL | MongoDB | Notes |
| ------------------------ | ---------- | ----- | ------- | ----- |
| Append-only write        | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |       |
| Hash chain integrity     | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |       |
| Tamper detection         | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |       |
| Cursor-based pagination  | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |       |
| Workspace-scoped queries | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |       |
| Time-range queries       | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |       |

## Change Streams

| Capability                          | PostgreSQL | MSSQL | MongoDB | Notes                                              |
| ----------------------------------- | ---------- | ----- | ------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| Real-time change events             | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      | Postgres: LISTEN/NOTIFY; MSSQL: CDC; Mongo: native |
| Workspace-filtered streams          | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |                                                    |
| Reconnect with resume token         | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |                                                    |
| At-least-once delivery              | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |                                                    |
| Ordered delivery (within workspace) | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |                                                    |

## Schema Introspection

| Capability                | PostgreSQL | MSSQL | MongoDB | Notes                                      |
| ------------------------- | ---------- | ----- | ------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| List collections/tables   | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |                                            |
| Describe schema           | ✅         | ✅    | ⚠️      | MongoDB: schema is inferred from documents |
| Detect schema drift       | ✅         | ✅    | ⚠️      | MongoDB: best-effort                       |
| Foreign key relationships | ✅         | ✅    | ⚠️      | MongoDB: advisory only                     |

## Search

| Capability                 | PostgreSQL | MSSQL | MongoDB | Notes                          |
| -------------------------- | ---------- | ----- | ------- | ------------------------------ |
| Full-text search           | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |                                |
| Case-insensitive filtering | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |                                |
| Regex filtering            | ✅         | ⚠️    | ✅      | MSSQL: PATINDEX-based; limited |

## Backup / Restore

| Capability            | PostgreSQL | MSSQL | MongoDB | Notes                                      |
| --------------------- | ---------- | ----- | ------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| Point-in-time restore | ✅         | ✅    | ⚠️      | MongoDB: oplog-based; requires replica set |
| Export / import       | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      |                                            |
| Encrypted backup      | ✅         | ✅    | ✅      | via Restic                                 |

---

## Capability Caveats

(Expand ⚠️ entries with detail)

**MongoDB multi-entity transactions:** Requires a replica set (single-node replica set is supported; standalone is not). The platform deployment guide documents this requirement. Attempting multi-entity transactions against a standalone Mongo instance returns a clear `TransactionUnsupportedError`.

**MongoDB pessimistic locking:** Emulated using `findAndModify` with a lock document. Not a native lock; lock TTL is 30 seconds. Held locks expire and are released; no deadlock risk.

**MongoDB schema introspection:** Schema is inferred by sampling the most recent 1,000 documents per collection. If the collection is empty or documents are heterogeneous, the inferred schema may be incomplete. The platform surfaces this as a capability caveat in the UI.

**MSSQL regex filtering:** Uses `PATINDEX` with SQL Server's pattern syntax, which is a subset of POSIX regex. Complex patterns (lookahead, lookahead, capture groups) are not supported. The platform translates unsupported patterns to a `CapabilityNotSupportedError`.

**MongoDB PITR:** Requires oplog access, which requires a replica set configuration. Standalone MongoDB deployments can only restore to a backup point (not arbitrary time).
